facebook.content_angle: Hip rotation — the hidden power source most club swimmers underuse, and how unlocking even 10 degrees more rotation translates directly into longer stroke, less drag, and faster splits
facebook.post_text:
Your hips are holding back your freestyle. 🔄

Most club swimmers generate power almost entirely from their arms — and wonder why they plateau despite grinding out the metres.

The real engine in freestyle isn't your pull. It's your hip rotation.

👉 Elite swimmers rotate 40–50° per stroke, creating a longer catch and reducing frontal drag simultaneously
👉 More rotation means your recovering arm travels further forward before entry — adding centimetres to every stroke length
👉 That extra reach compounds across a 100m — you're covering more water with the same energy output

Fewer strokes per length. Faster splits. Same lungs. 🔥

✔️ Drill to own it: Side-kick drill — hold a full rotated position for two kicks before each stroke. Force your body to feel the correct angle
✔️ Count your strokes per 25m today, then focus on rotation for four weeks and count again
✔️ Most club swimmers drop 1–2 strokes per 25m — that's a significant time gain at zero extra fitness cost

How many strokes are you currently taking per 25m?
